name: Mobile Toilet Cabin
description:
A mobile toilet cabin is a self-contained steel toilet cabin built to be set down and used at almost any location — an event ground, a remote field point, or a new site before plumbing arrives — then lifted onto a vehicle and taken to the next job when work moves on. It is one finished unit you own and position where you need it, not a structure fixed permanently into the ground.

What Is a Mobile Toilet Cabin, and How Is It Different From a Fixed Portable Toilet?

A mobile toilet cabin is a complete, free-standing steel toilet cabin designed to be placed and used at any location, then lifted and moved when the need ends. The difference from a fixed portable toilet is mobility by design: it sits on a level surface with no civil work, so you can deploy it wherever sanitation is suddenly required.

The orange mobile toilet cabin deployed at an open-air event for visitor sanitation.

That single property — drop it where you need it, take it when you're done — shapes the rest of this unit, from how it is built to how it is priced. It is one format within the full portable toilet range, and the section at the end of this page helps you place it against the two units it is most often confused with.

A Roomy Steel Cabin, Not a Moulded Cubicle

At 10 × 10 × 8 ft, this is a generous unit — closer to a small room than the single squeeze-in cubicle most people picture when they hear "portable toilet." Built as a finished steel cabin rather than a moulded plastic box, the extra footprint is the point: the same shell can be supplied as one roomy single unit or partitioned into a two-cubicle mobile cabin toilet, decided before dispatch.

A two-cubicle white mobile toilet cabin serving a society compound — a facility-management use.

The exterior is powder-coated in your choice of colour — white, green, red, sky-blue and more — so the unit can match an event's look or a company's site branding instead of standing out as bare plastic. Inside, a finished wood-effect interior gives it the feel of a proper room rather than a temporary box.

Putting It to Work: Water, Ventilation and the Ground It Stands On

The unit needs only a level, firm patch of ground — no foundation, no civil work — which is what makes it practical to drop onto an open plot. For water, you choose what suits the spot: a ½-inch inlet off a mains line, an overhead tank your team tops up, or a built-in septic tank for locations with no connection at all. A wall vent and a roof vent keep air moving so the cabin does not turn stale in summer heat.

A mobile toilet cabin in green providing worker sanitation inside a factory compound.

This is why a mobile toilet cabin for construction sites and events works where fixed sanitation can't — a launch venue, an exhibition ground, a remote survey team, or a fresh site waiting on its permanent plumbing.

What a Mobile Toilet Cabin Costs — and What That Price Covers

A SAMAN mobile toilet cabin is priced at ₹1,05,000 (regular ₹1,15,000, inclusive of taxes) for the standard 10 × 10 × 8 ft unit. That covers the steel cabin structure, a fitted pan, the water inlet, wall-and-roof ventilation, the lockable door, the finished interior, and the powder-coated exterior. What moves the number: a two-cubicle layout, a septic-tank build, the pan type you choose (western or Indian), or a custom exterior colour.

A white mobile toilet cabin off-loaded and levelled on site — same-day on an accessible spot.

Across India, a mobile toilet cabin is delivered from SAMAN's own facilities and, on an accessible site, off-loaded and levelled the same day.

When to Choose a Mobile Cabin, a Movable Unit, or a Truck-Movable Toilet

The name sits close to two other SAMAN units, so here is the quick way to tell which one fits your job.

Unit Best for How it gets positioned
Mobile toilet cabin (this page) One finished cabin you place and use anywhere — events, remote points, a new site Set on a level spot; lifted onto a vehicle and repositioned when the job ends
Movable toilet cabin A unit you reposition between locations as one project's work front advances Shifted along the work as it progresses
Mobile toilet A heavier unit for remote sites with difficult access Truck-mounted or crane-lifted into place

The mobile toilet cabin lifted onto a truck — how the unit is repositioned when a job ends.

If your toilet will follow the work — moving spot to spot along a road, a pipeline, or a phased build — then a unit built to relocate as the work moves is the better match. If access is the real problem and the unit has to be craned or driven into a difficult location, look at a truck- or crane-movable toilet instead.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a mobile toilet cabin made of — steel or plastic?

SAMAN's mobile toilet cabin is a steel-framed cabin with a finished interior and a powder-coated exterior — not a moulded plastic or FRP cubicle. The steel structure is what lets it handle daily use from a full crew across many deployments rather than a single season.

How much does a mobile toilet cabin cost, and can I buy one outright?

The standard 10 × 10 × 8 ft unit is ₹1,05,000 (regular ₹1,15,000, inclusive of taxes), and yes — it is sold as a unit you own, not only rented. The final figure depends on a single- or two-cubicle layout, the water arrangement, the pan type, and any custom colour.

Mobile toilet cabin vs movable toilet cabin — which one do I need?

Choose the mobile cabin when you want one finished unit to place and use at a location, then move when the job ends. Choose a movable unit when the toilet has to follow the work, repositioning between spots as a single project advances. Same family of products; the deciding factor is whether the unit stays put per location or travels with the work front.

Can a mobile toilet cabin be moved after it's set up?

Yes — that is the point of it. It sits on a level surface without being fixed down, so it can be lifted onto a vehicle and taken to the next location when you no longer need it where it stands.

Get a Quote

Tell us the location, the layout you want (single or two-cubicle), and your water arrangement, and SAMAN will send a mobile toilet cabin quote with delivery timing. Call +91 80886 85440 or send your requirement on WhatsApp at +91 97089 89937 for a same-day response.
short_description:
Mobile Toilet Cabin
10×10×8 ft · place & use anywhere
A self-contained steel toilet cabin you set down and use at any location — events, remote points, or a new site — then lift and move when the job ends.
Size / Capacity
10×10×8 ft · 1–2 cubicle
Frame / Panel
Steel cabin · wood-finish
Price / Delivery
₹1,05,000 · same-day fit
Floor / Relocations
Anti-skid · liftable
Water: mains / tank / septic
Get a Quote
